summaries.brief_summary: CI fixes for ocaml-lsp-server, odoc, sherlodoc, and macOS dependencies opened by Matthew Else.
summaries.activity_summary: Two new PRs address CI failures; a long-running draft PR on package patching infrastructure saw discussion.
summaries.activity:
- [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) opened [#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) **Fix the CI**, cherry-picking fixes from the dev branch to repair builds of **odoc**, **sherlodoc**, and a macOS-breaking sherlodoc upstream dependency; the PR also pins `lwt` and `utop` to the versions in this repo, pins `checkseum` to the tip of its repo (tracking upstream fix [ocaml/opam-repository#29747](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29747)), and backports a `cut`-fix patch for macOS
- [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) opened and then closed [#37](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/37) **fix ocaml-lsp-server** (incorporating [oxcaml/ocaml-lsp#10](https://github.com/oxcaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/10)), superseded by the broader [#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38)
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s draft PR [#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27) **Allow multiple versions of upstream patched packages** received new discussion on 2026-04-14; the PR proposes replacing the large `conflicts` clause in `ocaml-variants.5.2.0+ox` with an auto-generated `oxcaml-patch-guards` package and per-package `oxcaml-<pkg>` / `oxcaml-<pkg>-patches` guards produced by `layout.sh`
summaries.discussions_summary: Discussion on PR #27 explored whether layout.sh belongs in this repo or in internal release infrastructure.
summaries.notable_discussions: - In [#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27), [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) asked whether `layout.sh` should live in internal release infra rather than this public repo; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) noted that keeping it here makes it easier for external contributors to add patched package versions and that the unified branch already uses it to keep guards up to date
summaries.trends_summary: CI reliability and the patching infrastructure for upstream OxCaml-patched packages are the active focus areas.
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **CI stability** is an active concern: both [#37](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/37) and [#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) address build failures across odoc, sherlodoc, ocaml-lsp-server, and macOS-specific issues, suggesting the CI surface area for this overlay repository is still being stabilised
- **Package patching governance** is under design: [#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27) represents an ongoing effort to move from hand-edited `conflicts` lists to a generated, scriptable approach for managing which patched upstream packages are permitted in OxCaml switches
summaries.contributors_summary: Matthew Else drove CI fixes this week; David Allsopp's patching infrastructure draft continued to receive discussion.
summaries.contributors:
- [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) opened two PRs targeting CI failures: [#37](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/37) (closed as superseded) and [#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) (open, fixes odoc, sherlodoc, macOS, lwt, utop, checkseum)
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) is the author of the long-running draft [#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27) and engaged in discussion about the `layout.sh` placement question raised by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js)
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) contributed a design question in [#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27) about the appropriate home for the patch-guard generation script

summaries.brief_summary: Discussion on open PR #36 to update the bootstrap compiler to OCaml 5.4.1 for OxCaml main branch.
summaries.activity_summary: PR #36 to add OCaml 5.4.1 as bootstrap compiler remains open with active design discussion.
summaries.activity: - **Bootstrap compiler update to 5.4.1** proposed by [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) in [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) (opened 2026-03-31, updated this week) to keep the `ocaml-variants` opam file in sync with OxCaml's main branch; the PR modifies 2 files with 22 additions and 19 deletions and is awaiting resolution of workflow concerns raised in review
summaries.discussions_summary: Technical discussion in PR #36 about how updating the bootstrap compiler interacts with existing opam switch workflows.
summaries.notable_discussions:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) raised concerns in [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) that replacing the bootstrap compiler entry breaks the existing workflow for the `5.2.0minus-25` tag (which requires the `4.14.2` bootstrap), questioning how users would get a working version of `main` with this change
- [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) clarified that the intended workflow relies on `opam compiler create oxcaml:main` (from [ocaml-opam/opam-compiler#42](https://github.com/ocaml-opam/opam-compiler/issues/42)), which would discover the `+ox` version and pin `ocaml-variants` with source from `main`; testing showed `main` builds successfully producing `5.2.0+ox`
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) noted the underlying issue that OxCaml lacks an `ocaml-variants.opam` file (historically introduced for OCaml 4.08/4.09 in [ocaml/ocaml#8604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/8604)) and flagged the related tracking issue [oxcaml/oxcaml#5344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5344)
summaries.trends_summary: Ongoing work to align OxCaml's opam packaging with upstream OCaml release cadence and tooling workflows.
summaries.emerging_trends: - The discussion in [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) reflects a recurring challenge of keeping OxCaml's opam metadata synchronized with both the upstream OCaml release cycle and OxCaml-specific versioning schemes, with the `opam compiler` tool ([ocaml-opam/opam-compiler#42](https://github.com/ocaml-opam/opam-compiler/issues/42)) emerging as a key integration point for managing OxCaml switches
summaries.contributors_summary: Eduardo Souza opened PR #36 and responded to review; David Allsopp provided technical review feedback.
summaries.contributors:
- [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou) authored [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) to update the bootstrap compiler to 5.4.1 and responded to review feedback explaining the intended `opam compiler` workflow
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) reviewed [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36), raising workflow compatibility concerns and identifying the missing `ocaml-variants.opam` issue tracked in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5344](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5344)

summaries.brief_summary: js_of_ocaml package switched to track the upstream oxcaml branch directly, removing 47 individual patches.
summaries.new_features_summary: js_of_ocaml-compiler.6.0.1+ox now points to the oxcaml branch of js_of_ocaml upstream, eliminating a large local patch stack.
summaries.new_features:
- The `js_of_ocaml-compiler.6.0.1+ox` and `js_of_ocaml-ppx.6.0.1+ox` opam packages now source from the **oxcaml branch** of the upstream [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) repository (commit `c3da0bb`) rather than an older snapshot with local patches applied, enabling consumers to pick up OxCaml-specific fixes including parameterised library support needed for incremental jsoo compilation in dune ([ocaml/dune#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414)) ([#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31))
- 47 individual patch files (covering atomics, float32, unboxed indexing, labeled tuples, mixed blocks, and more) were **removed from the patch stack** as their changes are now present directly in the upstream oxcaml branch, simplifying long-term maintenance ([#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31))
summaries.activity_summary: PR #31, open since January 22, was merged, updating jsoo packages to track the oxcaml upstream branch directly.
summaries.activity: - [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) opened [#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31) on January 22 and it was merged this week, updating `js_of_ocaml-compiler.6.0.1+ox` and related packages to point to the **oxcaml branch** of js_of_ocaml rather than applying a growing set of patches on top of an older snapshot; the PR description notes the branch has accumulated too many changes to ship as individual patches and that pointing to the public branch is easier for the community to maintain
summaries.discussions_summary: PR #31 included a note cc'ing Jérôme Vouillon as the jsoo maintainer about the oxcaml branch approach.
summaries.notable_discussions: - [#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31) cc'd [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon), the upstream js_of_ocaml maintainer, as stakeholder in the decision to point directly to the public oxcaml branch rather than continuing to maintain a local patch series
summaries.trends_summary: Shift toward consuming upstream oxcaml-specific branches directly rather than maintaining local patches in this opam overlay.
summaries.emerging_trends: - The merge of [#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31) continues a pattern of **reducing the local patch burden** in this OxCaml opam overlay by moving OxCaml-specific changes upstream and then consuming them via branch or commit pins rather than carrying patches
summaries.contributors_summary: [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) authored the PR and [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) merged it.
summaries.contributors:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) authored [#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31), proposing and implementing the switch of `js_of_ocaml-compiler.6.0.1+ox` to track the upstream oxcaml branch and removing the accumulated local patch stack of 47 patches
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) merged [#31](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/31)

summaries.brief_summary: Added opam-core and opam-format packages with locality fixes; expanded CI testing to include odoc and sherlodoc
summaries.new_features_summary: Added opam-core and opam-format packages with locality type conversion patches for OxCaml compatibility
summaries.new_features:
- **New opam-core 2.5.0+ox package** with patches to fix locality type conversion issues where `fn : _ @ local -> _` expressions now work with explicit lambda wrappers `(fun x -> fn x)` for proper `@ global` casting ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33))
- **New opam-format 2.5.0+ox package** added as dependency for opam tooling, enabling proper format library support for opam 2.5 under OxCaml ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33))
- **CI testing expanded** to include odoc and sherlodoc packages in the automated installation verification workflow ([#34](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/34))
summaries.activity_summary: Fixed opam-core compilation errors and expanded CI coverage for documentation tools
summaries.activity:
- **Locality type conversion patches** applied to opam-core 2.5.0+ox addressing compilation failures where locality-annotated functions (`_ @ local -> _`) were not automatically cast to global context (`_ @ global -> _`), requiring explicit lambda wrappers as a quickfix by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33))
- **Documentation toolchain support** improved with [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) adding odoc and sherlodoc to CI package testing, resolving installation verification for oxcaml/odoc#22 compilation errors ([#34](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/34))
- **Upstream contribution discussion** initiated by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) suggesting these locality fixes should be fed back to upstream opam as quality of life improvements, similar to recent [ocaml/dune#13342](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13342) changes ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33))
summaries.trends_summary: Continued pattern of adapting OCaml ecosystem packages for OxCaml's locality type system with explicit conversion workarounds
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Locality type system integration** continues to require patches across the OCaml ecosystem, with opam-core joining the growing list of packages needing explicit type conversion workarounds for functions with locality annotations
- **Upstream contribution opportunities** emerging as OxCaml-specific fixes (like locality type conversions) could benefit the broader OCaml ecosystem when generalized, following the pattern established with the dune project
- **CI-driven quality assurance** expanding to cover documentation tooling (odoc, sherlodoc) alongside core development tools, ensuring the OxCaml fork maintains compatibility with the full OCaml toolchain
summaries.contributors_summary: Two contributors merged PRs with art-w fixing opam packages and mt_caret expanding CI testing; avsm provided reviews
summaries.contributors:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) implemented locality type conversion patches for opam-core and opam-format packages, creating new 2.5.0+ox versions with explicit lambda wrapper workarounds for global/local casting issues ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33))
- [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret) expanded the CI testing workflow to include odoc and sherlodoc in the automated package installation verification, addressing compilation errors in the documentation toolchain ([#34](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/34))
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) reviewed both PRs, merged them to the main branch, and provided guidance on contributing locality fixes upstream to the OCaml ecosystem ([#33](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/33), [#34](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/34))

summaries.brief_summary: Package updates with Dune 3.21.0+ox, Lwt 6.0.0+ox additions, build performance optimization, and new libraries added
summaries.new_features_summary: Dune 3.21.0+ox, Lwt 6.0.0+ox packages, compiler build concurrency fix, and new libraries including handled_effect
summaries.new_features:
- **Dune 3.21.0+ox packages** added with complete build toolchain including dune, dune-configurator, dune-build-info, dune-action-plugin, dune-rpc, dune-rpc-lwt, dune-site, dune-glob, dune-private-libs, chrome-trace, fs-io, stdune, dyn, ocamlc-loc, ordering, and top-closure ([#29](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/29))
- **Lwt 6.0.0+ox packages** including lwt, lwt_direct, lwt_ppx, and lwt_runtime_events, partially upstreamed to [ocsigen/lwt#1096](https://github.com/ocsigen/lwt/issues/1096) ([#30](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/30))
- Compiler **build concurrency optimization** with Dune package management by unsetting `INSIDE_DUNE` environment variable before building, reducing build time from 32 minutes to 14 minutes ([#25](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/25))
- **js_of_ocaml-toplevel build fix** by removing obsolete `[%%if]` patch that was causing extension errors ([#26](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/pull/26))
- **New libraries added**: handled_effect.v0.18~preview.130.83+317 (renamed from oxcaml_effect), memtrace.v0.18~preview.130.83+317, oxcaml_intrinsics.v0.18~preview.130.83+317, unboxed_datatypes.v0.18~preview.130.83+317, hardcaml_hobby_boards_demos, hardcaml_hobby_boards_kernel, and hardcaml_template_project
- **Package version updates** for over 100 Jane Street packages from v0.18~preview.130.76+222 to v0.18~preview.130.83+317 including core, async, bonsai, ppx_* packages, and related libraries
- **Build tooling updates**: ocamlfind 1.9.8+ox, notty-community 0.2.4+ox with title save/restore patch, and reduced patches for js_of_ocaml 6.0.1+ox, mdx 2.5.0+ox, re 1.14.0+ox, and utop 2.16.0+ox
- **CI configuration update** removing macOS 13 testing from GitHub workflows

summaries.brief_summary: Major package updates delivered including js_of_ocaml toplevel support, eio integration, and documentation tools

summaries.new_features:

- **Documentation toolchain** updates including odoc packages (odoc, odoc-driver, odoc-md, odoc-parser) and sherlodoc for enhanced documentation generation ([#8](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/8))
- **Utop upgrade** to version 2.16.0 with updated patches for improved REPL experience ([#10](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/10))
- **MDX package** updated with unit-info patches for literate programming support ([#9](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/9))

summaries.brief_summary: Added patched eio and odoc ecosystem packages for Oxcaml compatibility
summaries.new_features_summary: Added patched eio packages and complete odoc ecosystem with Oxcaml-specific patches
summaries.new_features:
- **eio ecosystem packages** added with Oxcaml-specific patches including eio, eio_linux, eio_main, and eio_posix for enhanced async I/O support ([#8](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/8))
- **Complete odoc documentation suite** integrated including odoc, odoc-driver, odoc-parser, odoc-md, and sherlodoc packages from oxcaml organization ([#8](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/8))
- **ocaml-lsp-server compatibility patch** added to work with latest odoc-parser version for improved editor support ([#8](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/8))
- **mdx package updates** with new unit-info patch and migration to git+https source URLs for better maintainability ([#9](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/9))
summaries.activity_summary: Two new PRs opened for package ecosystem integration with extensive collaboration on source URL management
summaries.activity:
- **Two new PRs opened** during the week by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam): odoc and eio integration ([#8](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/8)) and mdx patch updates ([#9](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/9)), both merged in early September
- **Package source strategy discussion** between [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) and [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) regarding git+https URLs versus patch files for easier maintenance ([#8](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/8))
- **Comprehensive package integration** work involving 15+ opam files added for the complete odoc documentation toolchain and eio async I/O library ecosystem
summaries.discussions_summary: Collaborative discussion on package maintenance strategy between direct git sources and patch files
summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Package maintenance strategy debate** in [#8](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/8) where [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) advocated for direct git+https URLs over patch files for easier maintenance, leading [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) to migrate from patch-based to direct repository sourcing approach
- **Source URL management discussion** highlighting the trade-offs between pointing to upstream repositories with patches versus maintaining forks within the oxcaml organization for a fast-moving ecosystem
summaries.contributors_summary: Jon Ludlam led package integration work with collaborative input from avsm on maintenance strategy
summaries.contributors:
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) authored both PRs during the week, implementing comprehensive integration of eio async I/O packages ([#8](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/8)) and mdx documentation tool updates ([#9](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/9)), along with multiple commits transitioning from patch-based to git+https source management
- [@avsm](https://github.com/avsm) provided technical guidance on package maintenance strategies in [#8](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/8), advocating for direct git repository sources over patch files for the fast-moving oxcaml ecosystem

summaries.brief_summary: Added CI workflow, fixed build issues, and completed package rebranding from Jane Street to OxCaml
summaries.new_features_summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow for multi-platform testing and ppxlib_ast build script fixes
summaries.new_features:
- **GitHub Actions CI workflow** added for testing package installation across multiple platforms including Ubuntu (x64, ARM64) and macOS (Intel, ARM64) with OxCaml compiler variant [#3](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/3)
- **ppxlib_ast build script fix** to use bash instead of sh, resolving build failures [#1](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/1), [#2](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/2)
- **Documentation improvements** with updated README featuring OxCaml branding, improved installation instructions, and comprehensive platform compatibility information [8a89fa2](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/8a89fa2def133816c885b75c1021f6e4218d25b8)
summaries.activity_summary: Three PRs merged focusing on CI infrastructure and build fixes by Puneeth Chaganti
summaries.activity:
- **CI infrastructure** established with [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan) adding GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing across Ubuntu and macOS platforms ([#3](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/3))
- **Build system fixes** delivered through two consecutive PRs fixing ppxlib_ast cleanup script execution by [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan) ([#1](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/1), [#2](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/2))

- **Documentation overhaul** transforming README from Jane Street to OxCaml branding with enhanced installation instructions and platform compatibility details
summaries.trends_summary: Repository transition from Jane Street to OxCaml branding and focus on CI automation
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Branding transition** from Jane Street to OxCaml with systematic package renaming across hundreds of packages using `+ox` suffix instead of `+jst`
- **CI automation emphasis** with implementation of comprehensive multi-platform testing infrastructure covering Ubuntu and macOS architectures
- **Build reliability improvements** through shell script compatibility fixes ensuring consistent builds across different environments
